Indicator: France MFI rate — new loans to households for house purchase
Notes: PROVISIONAL — Banque de France migrated from the legacy IBM API Connect stack (api.webstat.banque-france.fr/webstat-fr/v1) to an Opendatasoft Explore v2.1 instance at https://webstat.banque-france.fr/api/explore/v2.1/. The public catalogue exposes only one dataset (tableaux_rapports_preetablis = archived report attachments); the 37000+ time series including MFI lending rates are gated behind the developer-portal login. Once BDF_API_KEY is in the runtime: query the catalogue to locate the MFI interest-rate dataset_id (likely something like webstat-bdf-mir or mfi-interest-rates-france) browse its schema to find the SDMX dimension columns then replace this series_id with the form '<dataset_id>|<odsql_where>' encoding the original SDMX dot-key M.FR.B.A2C.A.R.A.2250.EUR.N (households for house purchase). Until verified the fetcher logs a PROVISIONAL warning and skips this row.

The public catalogue exposes only one dataset (tableaux_rapports_preetablis = archived report attachments); the 37000+ time series including MFI lending rates are gated behind the developer-portal login. Once BDF_API_KEY is in the runtime: query the catalogue to locate the MFI interest-rate dataset_id browse its schema to find the SDMX dimension columns then replace this series_id with the form '<dataset_id>|<odsql_where>' encoding the original SDMX dot-key M.FR.B.A2A.A.R.A.2240.EUR.N (non-financial corporations). Shiller's headline CAPE (P/E10) ratio computed from S&P 500 real price ÷ 10-year average real earnings. Sourced from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (http://www.econ.yale.edu/~shiller/data/ie_data.xls). Long-history monthly back to 1881. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — parser verified offline against the documented sheet layout; live cross-validation pending the first credentialed CI run (sandbox blocks Yale + the shillerdata.com / datahub.io / posix4e mirrors).

Monthly S&P Composite price level from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Long-history common-frame counterpart to daily SPX tickers; back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.
Monthly S&P Composite trailing dividend from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Monthly S&P Composite trailing earnings from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook. Back to Jan 1871. Used as the denominator anchor for the CAPE definition. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10 — cross-validation pending live CI run.

Long-run US CPI from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (BLS CPI-U linked to Warren-Pearson back to 1871). Pre-1913 portion is not BLS-native but Shiller's standard splice; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c, NOT a replacement for the modern BLS CUSR0000SA0 column (which remains the canonical USA_CPI_INDEX). Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Long-run US 10-year Treasury yield from the Yale ie_data.xls workbook (FRED GS10 spliced to NBER long-rate series pre-1953). Pre-1953 portion is not FRED-native; intended as a pre-WW2 cross-validation anchor for regime-AA Phase 0c. Wired §3.13 2026-06-10.

Kenneth French data library — US 5-factor monthly returns back to 1926-07. ZIP: https://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/ftp/F-F_Research_Data_5_Factors_2x3_CSV.zip. Wired §3.11 2026-06-10 — driven by regime-AA Phase 0 long-run factor requirement. International developed (Developed_5_Factors_CSV) and emerging (Emerging_5_Factors_CSV) extensions deferred as follow-up.

Conservative-minus-Aggressive investment factor from the F-F 5-Factor 2x3 monthly file. History from 1963-07.
One-month Treasury-bill return packaged with the F-F 5-Factor monthly file. History from 1926-07.
